# Orchestrator Checklist

End-to-end progression for the Agent Avila dual-truth fix and surrounding orchestrator work.

## ARC Governance Activation (ARC-GO-LIVE) — Active

ARC-GO-LIVE is a DOCS-ONLY governance activation step. It is committed and pushed at `de91d325b8160fb8183cc26172e50f3f35831796` and activates ARC-1 through ARC-7 as the active control layer for Agent Avila. It does NOT authorize live trading, production mutation, migration application, deploy, runtime edits, or any RED-tier action; subsequent RED-tier actions still require their own per-action explicit Victor / CEO approval.

- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-1 (`orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md`) is active as the per-path SAFE / RESTRICTED / HARD BLOCK classification matrix.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-2 (`orchestrator/APPROVAL-GATES.md`) is active as the 16-gate action-class matrix and "what is NOT operator approval" non-equivalence list.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-3 (`orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) is active as the six-mode phase-labeling system (READ-ONLY AUDIT / DESIGN-ONLY / DOCS-ONLY / SAFE IMPLEMENTATION / HIGH-RISK IMPLEMENTATION / PRODUCTION ACTION).
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-4 (`orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION-SELECTOR.md`) is active as the ten-rule next-action selector and master-order discipline.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-5 (`orchestrator/ROLE-HIERARCHY.md`) is active as the role hierarchy (Victor / ChatGPT / Gemini / Claude / Codex + future-automation governance-only inheritance) and prompt-template source.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-6 (`orchestrator/AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md`) is active as the GREEN / YELLOW / RED three-tier automation-permission system covering Claude, Codex, ChatGPT, Gemini, Ruflo, Relay, and any successor.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC-7 (`orchestrator/HANDOFF-RULES.md` + `orchestrator/handoffs/`) is active as the handoff packet rules and templates; packets cannot approve, cannot transport, and cannot trade.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: ARC governance is not live trading and is not production mutation. Trading-runtime separation per `ROLE-HIERARCHY.md` and `AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md` remains in force; the brains govern changes, they do not run the runtime.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: Victor / CEO remains the sole final authority. No AI role (Claude, Codex, ChatGPT, Gemini, Ruflo, Relay, successors) can self-approve. Codex PASS, clean `git status`, green tests, scheduled triggers, signed tokens, CI status, and LLM self-approval DO NOT satisfy any operator-approval gate.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: N-phase production work was confirmed blocked behind existing gates at activation; as of 2026-05-04, N-3 has advanced through the proper §11 gate via Attempt 6 SUCCESS at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877`, and Migration 008 is APPLIED to production. ARC-GO-LIVE did not bypass the N-3 gate; the gate was satisfied through the proper N-3 sequence.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: all six prior Victor production-action approvals (`9ae139d`, `3138e7f`, `e7bce59…` Attempt 3, `e7bce59…` Attempt 4, `726b2e3…` Attempt 5, `189eb1b…` Attempt 6 — succeeded) are CONSUMED and cannot be reused. Migration 009+ would require a separate in-session approval naming the exact full HEAD from `git rev-parse HEAD` at that future time, after a separate post-commit deploy-and-verify cycle.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: future work must obey ARC-1 through ARC-7 without exception (phase modes, protected files, approval gates, next-action selector, role hierarchy, automation permissions, handoff rules); next work returns to the N-track only via the normal ARC-4 selector and ARC-2 gates.
- [x] ARC-GO-LIVE: activation committed and pushed at `de91d325b8160fb8183cc26172e50f3f35831796`.

## Communication Hub — COMM-HUB-DOCS-A — Closed (COMM-HUB-DOCS-A-CLOSEOUT — Closed at `c6940209a1e1e40e620d91e17c416f8955da539e`)

COMM-HUB-DOCS-A is an operator-directed manual phase that wrote the official Agent Avila Communication Hub spec and templates. The Communication Hub is a Discord-centered control-room surface for project updates, approval requests, Codex warnings, summaries, and system alerts. It is operator-private, governance/comms layer only, and never enters the trading-runtime hot path. **COMM-HUB-DOCS-A closed at HEAD `728f979335617e78363cfdeb69e35807a401f352`; pushed to origin/main; Railway `agent-avila-dashboard` docs-only deployment successful/active via GitHub from `relentlessvic/agent-avila` branch `main` with deployment message `COMM-HUB-DOCS-A: Communication Hub spec + templates + cross-references`. The Communication Hub rulebook + templates are now part of Agent Avila. Relay remains DORMANT. No Discord server / bot / webhook / scheduler / MCP / cron installed. CEILING-PAUSE preserved; autopilot runtime DORMANT.**

## Communication Hub Install Design — COMM-HUB-DESIGN-DISCORD-INSTALL — Closed (Design-only PASS, no commit)

COMM-HUB-DESIGN-DISCORD-INSTALL was a DESIGN-ONLY operator-directed manual phase that produced an 11-section conversation-only design report for the click-by-click Discord install plan. The design report covered: phase purpose; Discord server name recommendation; 3-category 7-channel layout; channel topics; role/permission setup; @everyone restrictions; Category C DORMANT setup; manual install checklist for Victor; security settings; what remains DORMANT; what is explicitly NOT authorized; install risks; Codex review requirement; recommended next operator instruction. **Codex docs-only review returned PASS on all 15 questions with zero blocking issues, zero required edits, and explicit confirmation that the design is safe to use as the basis for a later separately-approved operator-driven manual install phase.** No commit was produced (design phase output is conversation-only). No file written. No Discord server / channel / role / invite / permission created. No bot / webhook / scheduler / MCP / cron / Relay / background automation installed. CEILING-PAUSE preserved. Autopilot runtime DORMANT. The design is now codified in `orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-INSTALL-DISCORD-CHECKLIST.md` by the COMM-HUB-DOCS-B-INSTALL-CHECKLIST phase below.

## Communication Hub Relay Install Checklist — COMM-HUB-DOCS-D-HERMES-INSTALL-CHECKLIST — Closed at `e18f2207eae4ab734beb6f29626de1a5e4cd5757`

COMM-HUB-DOCS-D-HERMES-INSTALL-CHECKLIST is an operator-directed manual DOCS-ONLY phase that codifies the click-by-click Relay Stage 5 install plan as a permanent on-disk checklist (`orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-INSTALL-RELAY-CHECKLIST.md`) so the install plan is version-controlled before any live Relay action. ## Communication Hub Relay Runtime Design — COMM-HUB-HERMES-RUNTIME-DESIGN — Closed (Design-only PASS, no commit)

COMM-HUB-HERMES-RUNTIME-DESIGN is a DESIGN-ONLY conversation-only operator-directed manual phase that produced the 18-section runtime design packet for Relay (the planned future Discord auto-publisher) at HEAD `01a449020cb97e817667557fadb2c80fc682479d`. The packet covers process model, language/framework selection (Node.js + discord.js v14), code location (separate `relentlessvic/agent-avila-hermes` GitHub repo recommendation), deployability (existing `agent-avila-hermes` Railway service), 9 allowed environment variables, forbidden environment variables, 3-layer network allowlist enforcement, file-based source-of-truth message store, strict JSON Schema, 11-gate pre-publish verification pipeline, idempotency mechanism, 28-class halt-on-anomaly state machine, structured-JSON logging discipline, dry-run / no-publish test mode reusing the canonical dry-run design fixtures, 3-layer Discord-read isolation proof, 3-layer trading-touch isolation proof, future implementation-phase file scope, forbidden-files list, 25 Codex review questions, 6 approval gates before coding, 9 approval gates before deployment, and the rollback path mirroring `COMM-HUB-HERMES-STAGE5-PARTIAL-INSTALL-RECORD.md` §7 verbatim. Codex docs-only review returned PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS verdict; EDIT-1 through EDIT-5 were applied verbatim to §5 (language-selection rationale + extended forbidden-deps list including modern cloud SDK families), §8 (env var count corrected from 8 to 9), §13 (verification gate sequence extended from 10 to 11 with new gate 9 = network allow-list / egress-anomaly verification mapping to canonical halt class 6), and §18.8 (rollback path aligned verbatim with partial-install record §7). Codex re-review returned final PASS on all 25 questions. The conversation-only design produced no commit; this current `COMM-HUB-DOCS-G-HERMES-RUNTIME-DESIGN-SPEC` phase IS the codification step.

## D-5.12f Live SELL_ALL Design Spec — D-5.12f-LIVE-SELLALL-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `5165b555a3de215d18f50c758393fd6ae8479019`

D-5.12f-LIVE-SELLALL-DESIGN-SPEC is a DOCS-ONLY operator-directed phase persisting the Codex-PASS-verified D-5.12f live SELL_ALL DB-first persistence design as a permanent on-disk SAFE-class design record at `orchestrator/handoffs/D-5-12F-LIVE-SELLALL-DESIGN.md` (445 lines). The codification reproduces the round-2 design with the M2 Codex required edit applied (call-site fallback no longer mutates `attempted_payload` by appending `attempted_payload_hash`; `attempted_payload` remains the pure 9-field redacted object byte-stable with helper-internal redaction at `dashboard.js:813-823`; `attempted_payload_hash` carried only as separate top-level variable / emergency-audit-row field). The design defaults are DB-driven SELL_ALL quantity via `loadOpenPosition("live")`, pre-Kraken DB gate required (null → throw `"No open live position to sell"` BEFORE Kraken), wallet deficit (wallet < db − 1e-8) → hard throw pre-Kraken, wallet excess (wallet > db + 1e-8) → structured `log.warn` and proceed selling DB-tracked quantity only, `fetchKrakenBalance()` retained pre-Kraken solely for drift check (`bal?.error` → hard throw), `shadowRecordManualLiveClose` reused as-is, `exitEntry.exitReason = "MANUAL_SELLALL"`, `failureContext.source = "manual_live_sellall"`, `balanceCache = null` on every post-Kraken outcome (Codex v2 option (b)), `position.json` written ONLY after DB success, no JSON fallback on DB failure, no auto-retry, no Kraken cancellation, pointer-only operator-visible thrown errors, `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` Layer 1 / Layer 2 gates unchanged, typed-CONFIRM UI gate unchanged, Migration 008 treated as APPLIED at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877`. The future code-phase touch perimeter is `dashboard.js` SELL_ALL block ONLY (estimated ~140-155 ins / ~10 del); no helper / migration / DB / `bot.js` / `scripts/` / `package*` / `position.json` / orchestrator/* changes (other than separate DOCS-ONLY closeout). Scope: 4 files — 1 new SAFE-class design record `orchestrator/handoffs/D-5-12F-LIVE-SELLALL-DESIGN.md` + 3 status doc updates `orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`. The shipped D-5.12e mutation at `dashboard.js:2138-2139` is NOT fixed by this phase; it is recorded inside the new design record as a separately-gated deferred future cleanup phase named D-5.12e.1 — emergency payload shape cleanup. The Relay runtime design template, Stage 5 partial-install record, Stage 5 preconditions doc, dry-run design template, Relay spec, Relay install checklist, Discord install checklist, channel layout, all existing COMM-HUB message templates, autopilot template set, and all safety-policy docs remain unchanged.

## ARC-8 Unpause — ARC-8-UNPAUSE — Closed at `22ba4a7663e22df64b7cc8e3c0324cbffc0e28f6`

ARC-8-UNPAUSE is a DOCS-ONLY operator-directed phase recording an explicit operator-initiated direction change per `orchestrator/AUTOPILOT-RULES.md` "Phase-loop ceiling and approval-fatigue mitigation" rule. The Phase-loop ceiling fired at 3 of 3 at completion of ARC-8-RUN-B; CEILING-PAUSE has been in effect since. ARC-8-UNPAUSE BREAKS CEILING-PAUSE via this commit and resets the phase-loop counter from 3 of 3 to 0 of 3 for a new supervised Autopilot cycle scoped to dashboard stability design. The new 11-phase master order binds for this cycle: 1 ARC-8-UNPAUSE → 2 ARC-8-RUN-C-DASHBOARD-STABILITY-DESIGN → 3 DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT → 4 DASH-2-UI-STABILITY-CLEANUP → 5 DASH-3-POSITION-DISPLAY-CANONICALIZATION → 6 DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP → 7 DASH-5-LIVE-CONTROLS-DESIGN-ONLY → 8 D-5.12f-LIVE-SELLALL-IMPLEMENTATION → 9 D-5.12e.1-EMERGENCY-PAYLOAD-CLEANUP → 10 DASH-6-FULL-DASHBOARD-SMOKE-HARNESS → 11 ARC-8-RUN-D-AUTOPILOT-BUILD-LOOP. Only Phase 1 (this) is authorized by this commit. Phases 2–11 are each independently gated under their own mode label, Codex review, and per-phase Victor approval. ARC-8-UNPAUSE rolls in two stale-tail closeout records whose substantive back-fill commits had already landed but whose journal headers had not been flipped: COMM-HUB-DOCS-G-HERMES-RUNTIME-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT-SYNC-CLOSEOUT now CLOSED at `17103c27597365d1c3132d8a7d05b1aaf1f49dcb`; D-5.12f-LIVE-SELLALL-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT-SYNC now CLOSED at `d90d19a64a9459e12e9eeb8b8e2916b68b6dfeaa`. Scope: 3 status docs only (`orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`). No safety-policy doc is modified. `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION-SELECTOR.md` master-order table is NOT edited (operator override binds in scope per its §"Operator override"). `orchestrator/AUTOPILOT-RULES.md` is NOT edited (the Phase-loop ceiling rule is satisfied by recording the operator direction change in the journal). No runtime / migration / script / package / lockfile / `.nvmrc` / `.env*` / `position.json` / deploy-config file is touched. The CEILING-PAUSE break does NOT activate the autopilot runtime — runtime activation is the substance of the separately-gated future Phase 11 ARC-8-RUN-D-AUTOPILOT-BUILD-LOOP at the end of the new master order. Relay remains shelved (passive bot member of `Agent Avila Hub` with `System-Writer` role + canonical channel overrides; no runtime running; no posting capability). Stage 5 Gate-10 install approval at `40f3137e842cd60acf1adf17ecc7fe2f0b1b8935` CONSUMED record is preserved. Migration 008 remains APPLIED to production at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877`. N-3 remains CLOSED. Approvers remain exactly `{Victor}`.

## ARC-8-RUN-C Dashboard Stability Design — ARC-8-RUN-C-DASHBOARD-STABILITY-DESIGN — Closed (Design-only PASS, no commit by the design phase itself)

ARC-8-RUN-C-DASHBOARD-STABILITY-DESIGN is a DESIGN-ONLY (Mode 2 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that produced the conversation-only dashboard stability roadmap covering: current state summary; next-phase verification against `NEXT-ACTION-SELECTOR.md` rules 1–10; dashboard stability risk map R1–R10 (Postgres↔position.json drift, stale browser/PWA cache, API response shape divergence, mode-switch race, manual control safety surface mismatch, emergency audit gaps, paper-control failure surfacing, smoke/regression coverage gap, multiple HTML-generating surfaces fragility, capital/performance JSON sidecars); proposed DASH-1→DASH-6 roadmap with mode labels, scope perimeters, Codex review requirements, and approval gates; hard-blocked surfaces during the DESIGN-ONLY phase, across the entire DASH track, on production-side surfaces, and at out-of-scope phase boundaries (Phase 8 D-5.12f impl, Phase 9 D-5.12e.1 cleanup, Phase 11 ARC-8-RUN-D autopilot build-loop); a Codex design-only review packet with 24 verification checks (A1–A4 phase legality, B1–B3 risk-map completeness, C1–C6 roadmap order safety, D1–D4 hard-blocked-surface compliance, E1–E8 forbidden-content compliance, F1–F3 cross-doc consistency); plain-English explanation. Codex round-1 (2026-05-07) returned PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS on A1, A4, B1, C3 (4 of 24 checks). Codex round-2 (2026-05-07) returned clean PASS on all 24 checks (Codex enumerated 28; same set), zero required edits. The phase produced no working-tree edit and no commit; the design output is conversation-only.

## DASH-1 Read-State Audit — DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT — Closed (READ-ONLY AUDIT verdict, no commit by the audit phase itself)

DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT is a READ-ONLY AUDIT (Mode 1 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that produced the conversation-only complete dashboard inventory across 15 sub-sections: state-source inventory, API route inventory (30 endpoints), HTML generator inventory (9 surfaces with corrected line ranges), front-end fetch / poll / SSE inventory (34 fetch sites + 14 setIntervals + 26 setTimeouts + 1 SSE + 4 WebSockets), render pipeline inventory (30 render functions; renderPosition defined twice), Postgres usage inventory (32 db.js exports + usage counts), `position.json` usage inventory (5 reads + 5 writes — 3 of which bypass DB), JSON sidecar inventory (6 ephemeral sidecars on Railway), Kraken / live-balance usage inventory, `bot_control` / control-state usage inventory (DB-first via `loadControl()` with JSON shadow + 24+ command cases), paper manual-control path inventory (5 paths, all DB-first), live manual-control path inventory (5 paths, 3 of which bypass DB), emergency-audit path inventory (4-layer ladder), browser / PWA / cache / stale-state risk inventory (no service worker; 14-poll overlap; renderPosition drift; `.env` first-boot writes), existing tests / smoke coverage inventory (3 Playwright specs covering 3 routes; 6 routes uncovered; zero JSON-shape contract assertions). The audit corrects three concrete factual errors in the previously-persisted `orchestrator/handoffs/ARC-8-RUN-C-DASHBOARD-STABILITY-DESIGN.md` (F1 — legacy `const HTML` actual range is `dashboard.js:3163-7735` not `:3163-13002`, verified by `grep -n 'html>\`;$' dashboard.js` returning 7 closing-`</html>\`;` lines; F2 — three live manual-control paths bypass DB persistence (live SELL_ALL + live SET_STOP_LOSS + live SET_TAKE_PROFIT), not just live SELL_ALL; F3 — six JSON sidecars are ephemeral on Railway, not just two) and surfaces three new gaps (G5.2 — live SET_STOP_LOSS bypasses DB, helper `shadowRecordManualLiveSLUpdate` exists at `dashboard.js:840` but is unwired from the live SL path at `:2275-2280`; G5.3 — live SET_TAKE_PROFIT bypasses DB, helper `shadowRecordManualLiveTPUpdate` exists at `:900` but is unwired from the live TP path at `:2301-2306`; G5.4 — live BUY/OPEN_LONG at `:1955-1957` may share the D-5.12e 10-key emergency-payload mutation pattern and must be re-audited by DASH-5) plus a DASH-6 scope refinement (DASH-6 must extend the existing `tests/` Playwright surface, not build from zero). The phase produced no working-tree edit and no commit; the audit output is conversation-only.

## DASH-2.A — Add deploy identity to health response — DASH-2.A — Closed at `d6c77af3f203d1e17f6238eb53a7232592dd670d`

DASH-2.A is a SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that adds deploy-identity observability to the existing `/api/health` JSON response. Scope: 1 file (`dashboard.js`); +12 ins / 0 del; additive only. The change adds 4 module-level constants (`DEPLOY_COMMIT_SHA`, `DEPLOY_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA`, `DEPLOY_DEPLOYMENT_ID`, `DEPLOY_BOOT_TIME`) resolved once at module init from `process.env.RAILWAY_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` and `process.env.RAILWAY_DEPLOYMENT_ID` with `?? null` fallback at `dashboard.js:88-92`, and a `deploy: { commitSha, commitShortSha, deploymentId, bootTime }` object field in the `/api/health` response object at `dashboard.js:12421-12426` (between the existing `database` field and the legacy-compat `krakenOk` field). All other `/api/health` fields byte-stable. The operator-granted scoped lift on RESTRICTED `dashboard.js` was granted for DASH-2.A only and CONSUMED at commit per `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md:59`. First runtime-code commit in the post-CEILING-PAUSE supervised cycle. Addresses DASH-1 R2 / G2.3 (deploy-identity visibility on /api/health). DASH-2.A is the foundation that DASH-3 (front-end version-drift pill), DASH-6 (smoke-harness contract assertion), and Phase 11 (autopilot deploy-freshness check) can build on without re-inventing.

## DASH-2-A-CLOSEOUT-SYNC — Roll in DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT-SPEC + DASH-2.A stale-tail closures — DASH-2-A-CLOSEOUT-SYNC — Closed at `dbdda33e9bb4608e7a19d225f872a73d7146db69`

DASH-2-A-CLOSEOUT-SYNC is a DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase rolling in two stale-tail closures whose substantive commits had already landed but whose journal headers had not been flipped: DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT-SPEC closed at `dcf453acf5d8ee281646cacb07810dcfe5d2850a` and DASH-2.A closed at `d6c77af3f203d1e17f6238eb53a7232592dd670d`. Scope: 3 status docs (`orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`). The DASH-1 audit report `orchestrator/handoffs/DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT.md` is NOT modified by this closeout-sync. The ARC-8-RUN-C design record `orchestrator/handoffs/ARC-8-RUN-C-DASHBOARD-STABILITY-DESIGN.md` is NOT modified. No safety-policy doc modified. The runtime file `dashboard.js` is NOT modified (DASH-2.A's lift was consumed at commit; no new lift granted by this closeout-sync). Same scope shape as the ARC-8-UNPAUSE roll-in (which consolidated two stale-tail closures in a single commit per the operator's chosen pattern).

This phase exists because DASH-1-READ-STATE-AUDIT-SPEC's commit at `dcf453a…` recorded itself as "in progress" in the journal preamble (and never received a follow-up flip), and DASH-2.A's commit at `d6c77af…` was correctly scoped to `dashboard.js` only (the operator-granted scoped lift on RESTRICTED dashboard.js would not have covered STATUS.md / CHECKLIST.md / NEXT-ACTION.md edits in that commit, so journal updates were deferred to a separate DOCS-ONLY phase). Codex's DASH-3 design round-1 review caught the journal staleness (A2 / A3 FAIL) and refused to PASS until the canonical journal was brought into alignment with the actual git history.

## DASH-3-POSITION-DISPLAY-CANONICALIZATION-DESIGN — Closed (Design-only PASS, no commit by the design phase itself)

DASH-3-POSITION-DISPLAY-CANONICALIZATION-DESIGN is a DESIGN-ONLY (Mode 2 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that produced the conversation-only design proposal for the dashboard's position-display canonicalization track addressing DASH-1 audit gaps G3.1 (`position.json` startup read; surface drift visibly when DB ↔ JSON disagree), G3.2 (all 5 dashboard data routes use the same Postgres-first position read), G3.3 (two `renderPosition` definitions need unified data shape contract), G3.4 (mode-mismatch detection across 9 HTML generators). The design proposes a sub-phase split: DASH-3.A (this design + the codification at DASH-3-POSITION-DISPLAY-CANONICALIZATION-DESIGN-SPEC), DASH-3.B (deferred — server-side payload contract normalization at `dashboard.js:1272-1275`; consolidates `getApiData`'s direct `position.json` JSON-fallback to delegate to `modeScopedSummary`), and DASH-3.C (deferred — server-side drift detection in `modeScopedSummary` at `:1378-1417` + v2 `renderPosition` drift badge at `:10797-10815`; legacy `renderPosition` at `:5453` inside `const HTML` template `:3169-7741` NOT touched due to template-escape risk). The phase produced no working-tree edit and no commit; the design output is conversation-only.

- [x] DASH-3-POSITION-DISPLAY-CANONICALIZATION-DESIGN: DASH-3.C touch perimeter identified at `dashboard.js:1378-1417` (live-mode drift detection in `modeScopedSummary`; ~15-25 ins, 0 del) + `dashboard.js:10797-10815` (v2 `renderPosition` drift badge; ~5-10 ins, 0 del); legacy `renderPosition` at `:5453` explicitly excluded due to template-escape risk inside `const HTML` template literal at `:3169-7741`.

## DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN — Closed (Design-only PASS, no commit by the design phase itself)

DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN is a DESIGN-ONLY (Mode 2 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that produced the conversation-only design proposal for the dashboard's paper-mode manual-control envelope cleanup track. The design verifies all 5 paper handlers DB-first (BUY at `dashboard.js:1867-1900`, CLOSE_POSITION at `:2050-2074`, SELL_ALL at `:2227-2251`, SET_STOP_LOSS at `:2265-2280`, SET_TAKE_PROFIT at `:2290-2306`) via `loadOpenPosition("paper")` + `shadowRecordManualPaperBuy/Close/SLUpdate/TPUpdate` helpers; identifies envelope inconsistency I1 (paper CLOSE_POSITION lacks `quantity` while paper SELL_ALL includes it; both close paper positions) as the single concrete envelope drift, with I2/I3/I4 classified as defensible asymmetries / lower priority; proposes a sub-phase split DASH-4.A (1-line additive change at `:2074` adding `quantity` to paper CLOSE_POSITION return for envelope symmetry with paper SELL_ALL at `:2251`; LOW risk; addresses I1) as the narrowest safe sub-phase, with DASH-4.B (SL/TP structured price fields; addresses I3) and DASH-4.C (structured errorCode envelope; addresses I4) deferred indefinitely. The phase produced no working-tree edit and no commit; the design output is conversation-only.

- [x] DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN: phase mode label DESIGN-ONLY (Mode 2 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) applied before any conversation output that mutated session state.
- [x] DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN: paper-handler DB-first verification performed via direct read of `dashboard.js` at HEAD `5d0abcb…`; all 5 paper handlers verified DB-first; all 5 throw on DB failure with `Manual paper {BUY|CLOSE|SELL_ALL|SL update|TP update} not recorded in DB` pattern; same `log.warn("d-5.7.1 dual-write", ...)` label across all 5.
- [x] DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN: envelope inconsistency I1 (paper CLOSE_POSITION at `:2074` lacks `quantity` while paper SELL_ALL at `:2251` includes it) identified as concrete envelope drift; `quantity` const already declared at `:2056` (CLOSE) and `:2233` (SELL_ALL); both call same persistence helper `shadowRecordManualPaperClose`.
- [x] DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN: I2 (paper BUY returns no P&L fields — defensible since P&L at entry = 0), I3 (SL/TP responses have only `ok` + `message` — UI doesn't currently consume entryPrice / new SL/TP price as fields), I4 (error throw messages have wording drift but consistent shape; no structured `errorCode` field) classified as defensible asymmetries / lower priority.
- [x] DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN: exit-entry helper input shape between CLOSE and SELL_ALL verified to differ only in `exitReason` (`MANUAL_CLOSE` vs `MANUAL_SELLALL`) and `orderId` prefix (`PAPER-SELL-` vs `PAPER-SELLALL-`); all other fields identical; no structural drift.

## DASH-4.A-PAPER-CLOSE-QUANTITY-ENVELOPE-FIX — Closed at `5e1509eebffacd7b73172367a157baadd9552df5`

DASH-4.A-PAPER-CLOSE-QUANTITY-ENVELOPE-FIX is a SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that adds the `quantity` field to the paper CLOSE_POSITION return object so it matches paper SELL_ALL's envelope shape. Scope: 1 file (`dashboard.js`); +1 ins / -1 del; additive only. The change inserts `quantity, ` between `orderId: exitOrderId, ` and `pnlPct, pnlUSD` at `dashboard.js:2074` (paper CLOSE_POSITION return statement, inside `if (command === "CLOSE_POSITION") { if (isPaper) { ... } }` block). The `quantity` const is `parseFloat(dbPos.quantity)` already declared at `:2056` and already passed to `shadowRecordManualPaperClose` via `exitEntry` at `:2061`, so the field value is identical across both routes. The new envelope `{ ok, message, price, orderId, quantity, pnlPct, pnlUSD }` is byte-symmetric with paper SELL_ALL's envelope at `dashboard.js:2251`. The operator-granted scoped lift on RESTRICTED `dashboard.js` was granted for DASH-4.A only and CONSUMED at commit per `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md:59`. Second runtime-code commit in the post-CEILING-PAUSE supervised cycle after DASH-2.A at `d6c77af…`. Addresses I1 from DASH-4 design (`orchestrator/handoffs/DASH-4-PAPER-CONTROLS-CLEANUP-DESIGN.md` §4.2).

## DASH-5-LIVE-CONTROLS-DESIGN-ONLY — Closed (Design-only PASS, no commit by the design phase itself)

DASH-5-LIVE-CONTROLS-DESIGN-ONLY is a DESIGN-ONLY (Mode 2 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that produced the conversation-only design proposal for the dashboard's live manual-control surface across all 5 G5.x gaps from DASH-1 audit. The design verifies all 5 live handlers by direct read of `dashboard.js` at HEAD `c8b0e3c…`: live SELL_ALL DB-bypass at `:2253-2261` (owned by Phase 8); live SET_STOP_LOSS DB-bypass at `:2281-2286` with helper `shadowRecordManualLiveSLUpdate` at `:846-903` complete-but-unwired (DASH-5.A); live SET_TAKE_PROFIT DB-bypass at `:2307-2312` with helper `shadowRecordManualLiveTPUpdate` at `:906-964` complete-but-unwired (DASH-5.B); live BUY at `:1956-1975` clean of D-5.12e mutation pattern (G5.4 closed as no-finding by direct re-audit); live CLOSE_POSITION mutation at `:2145` (owned by Phase 9). The phase produced no working-tree edit and no commit; the design output is conversation-only.

## DASH-5.A-LIVE-STOP-LOSS-HELPER-WIRING — Closed at `5683a5a76c5094827be8a3bae8c04c599a85bf36`

DASH-5.A-LIVE-STOP-LOSS-HELPER-WIRING is a SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that wires the existing complete helper `shadowRecordManualLiveSLUpdate(dbPos, newSL)` at `dashboard.js:846-903` into the live `SET_STOP_LOSS` caller. Scope: 1 file (`dashboard.js`); +14 ins / -6 del; the live SET_STOP_LOSS branch's prior 6-line direct `position.json` write block (was `:2281-2286`) is replaced with a 14-line paper-symmetric DB-canonical block (post-edit `:2281-2294`). The new flow mirrors paper SL at `:2265-2280` byte-for-byte modulo `"live"`/`"paper"` mode strings, helper name (`shadowRecordManualLiveSLUpdate` vs `shadowRecordManualPaperSLUpdate`), and log label (`"d-5.12c live-helper"` matching the existing live-helper convention at `:890` vs `"d-5.7.1 dual-write"` for paper): `loadOpenPosition("live")` → throw-if-no-DB-row → `newSL` from `dbPos.entry_price` → `await shadowRecordManualLiveSLUpdate(dbPos, newSL)` → throw-on-`!r.ok` with reason → return. The operator-granted scoped lift on RESTRICTED `dashboard.js` was granted for DASH-5.A only and CONSUMED at commit per `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md:59`. Third runtime-code commit in the post-CEILING-PAUSE supervised cycle (after DASH-2.A at `d6c77af…` and DASH-4.A at `5e1509e…`). Addresses G5.2 from DASH-1 audit per the canonical DASH-5 design (`orchestrator/handoffs/DASH-5-LIVE-CONTROLS-DESIGN.md` §3 / §8).

## DASH-5.B-LIVE-TAKE-PROFIT-HELPER-WIRING — Closed at `9eb21f8f9ac73a452ff5822fdeb05029bf642da8`

DASH-5.B-LIVE-TAKE-PROFIT-HELPER-WIRING is a SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase that wires the existing complete helper `shadowRecordManualLiveTPUpdate(dbPos, newTP)` at `dashboard.js:906-964` into the live `SET_TAKE_PROFIT` caller. Scope: 1 file (`dashboard.js`); +14 ins / -6 del; the live SET_TAKE_PROFIT branch's prior 6-line direct `position.json` write block (was `:2315-2320` pre-edit) is replaced with a 14-line paper-symmetric DB-canonical block (post-edit `:2315-2328`). The new flow mirrors paper TP at `:2298-2313` byte-for-byte modulo `"live"`/`"paper"` mode strings, helper name (`shadowRecordManualLiveTPUpdate` vs `shadowRecordManualPaperTPUpdate`), and log label (`"d-5.12c live-helper"` matching DASH-5.A's convention vs `"d-5.7.1 dual-write"` for paper): `loadOpenPosition("live")` → throw-if-no-DB-row → `newTP` from `dbPos.entry_price` → `await shadowRecordManualLiveTPUpdate(dbPos, newTP)` → throw-on-`!r.ok` with reason → return. The operator-granted scoped lift on RESTRICTED `dashboard.js` was granted for DASH-5.B only (operator's directive: "Touch dashboard.js only. Only touch the live SET_TAKE_PROFIT branch") and CONSUMED at commit per `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md:59`. Fourth runtime-code commit in the post-CEILING-PAUSE supervised cycle (after DASH-2.A at `d6c77af…`, DASH-4.A at `5e1509e…`, and DASH-5.A at `5683a5a…`). Mirror of DASH-5.A's SL-helper-wiring change. Addresses G5.3 from DASH-1 audit per the canonical DASH-5 design (`orchestrator/handoffs/DASH-5-LIVE-CONTROLS-DESIGN.md` §4 / §8).

## DASH-5.D-LIVE-MANUAL-CONTROL-FAILURE-TAXONOMY-CODIFICATION — Closed at `f2913dfe218944232b8a85f79d497512e2a40391`

DASH-5.D-LIVE-MANUAL-CONTROL-FAILURE-TAXONOMY-CODIFICATION is a DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3 per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`) operator-directed phase codifying the 7-class failure taxonomy from `DASH-5-LIVE-CONTROLS-DESIGN.md` §6 as a permanent on-disk SAFE-class reference at `orchestrator/handoffs/LIVE-MANUAL-CONTROL-FAILURE-TAXONOMY.md`. Scope: 4 files — 1 new SAFE-class taxonomy reference + 3 status doc updates (`orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`). The new reference distinguishes 3 `kraken_post_success_*` classes (post-Kraken DB-failure; real money moved; emergency-audit ladder routing) from 4 `db_only_*` classes (pre-Kraken DB-only failure; no Kraken interaction; throw-on-DB-failure ladder collapses). ## D-5.12e.1-EMERGENCY-PAYLOAD-CLEANUP — Closed at `5273005a3df155b8fd58ddef49d9b30a8107a7f0`

D-5.12e.1-EMERGENCY-PAYLOAD-CLEANUP was Phase 9 of the post-CEILING-PAUSE 11-phase master order. Mode 5 HIGH-RISK IMPLEMENTATION by structural rule per `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md:117 + :134 + :138` (touches live CLOSE_POSITION handler path executed when `paperTrading === false`). Behavioral risk Mode-4-equivalent (Path C reconciliation): forensic byte-stability fix only; no Kraken / DB / position / order / `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` / operator-controls / live-trading execution change. The phase removed the inherited D-5.12e M2 byte-stability mutation that D-5.12f §10 had explicitly deferred to D-5.12e.1. The change was a strict 1-line deletion at `dashboard.js:2145` (pre-edit): `attempted_payload.attempted_payload_hash = attempted_payload_hash;`. After the deletion, `attempted_payload` remains a pure 9-field `_redactAttemptedPayload` return value matching the bytes that `attempted_payload_hash` (separate top-level variable) covers, closing the M2 byte-stability gap. Brings the CLOSE_POSITION else-branch into M2 parity with the M2-clean reference at the SELL_ALL else-branch (D-5.12f at `dashboard.js:2317-2339`); all four live failure-ladder sites (SET_STOP_LOSS, CLOSE_POSITION, SET_TAKE_PROFIT, SELL_ALL) are now M2-clean.

No optional comment block added (operator-rejected). SELL_ALL divergence-citing comment at `dashboard.js:2336-2338` (post-D-5.12f line numbering) NOT touched (operator-deferred per design §6). No other dashboard.js range touched.

## DASH-6.A — Closed at `f1f317f93d1343c167a9c6a4219ded490ba0aa5e`

DASH-6.A was Phase 10.A of the post-CEILING-PAUSE 11-phase master order. Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (test-only addition; `tests/` not in `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md` HARD BLOCK or RESTRICTED list; no live trading path; no real-money behavior change; no `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` action). Closes gap G1 from the DASH-6 design (Codex DESIGN-ONLY round-5 PASS). The phase added a single new file `tests/dash-6-health.spec.js` (116 lines) covering /api/health smoke assertions: response 200 + Content-Type: application/json; full top-level health envelope (success, kraken, websocket, bot, lastRun, lastRunAge, krakenLatency, serverTime, errors, krakenOk); persistence sub-shape (D-5.2); database sub-shape (D-5.4 / D-5.7.2); deploy-identity sub-shape (DASH-2.A — the gap G1 target: commitSha, commitShortSha, deploymentId, bootTime); defense-in-depth `page.context().route` interception on `api.kraken.com` capturing any browser-context request and asserting the captured list is empty.

- [x] DASH-6.A: documented Path A exception verified — the /api/health handler at `dashboard.js:12385` performs a server-side fetch to the public Kraken Time endpoint. This call is made by the dashboard subprocess (started by Playwright's webServer config); Playwright route interception in the test process cannot reach the dashboard subprocess's outbound network. The call is permitted as a low-risk exception because Kraken's /0/public/Time endpoint is public, unauthenticated, read-only, never uses `KRAKEN_API_KEY` / `KRAKEN_SECRET_KEY`, and never queries wallet, balance, position, order, SL/TP, SELL_ALL, or trading data — identical to what production /api/health performs on every probe.

## DASH-6.C — Closed at `0e93f5678d262679cf66ea8361a3bcc8b41d95a7`

DASH-6.C was Phase 10.C of the post-CEILING-PAUSE 11-phase master order. Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (test-only addition; `tests/` not in `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md` HARD BLOCK or RESTRICTED list; paper mode only; no live trading path; no real-money behavior change; no `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` action). Closes gap G3 from the DASH-6 design (Codex DESIGN-ONLY round-5 PASS). The phase added a single new file `tests/dash-6-paper-controls.spec.js` (~202 lines) covering paper /api/trade dispatch smoke assertions: non-prod `DATABASE_URL` hard-abort guard with two-source check (process.env + .env, both inspected independently per Codex IMPL round-1 RE on C2/S3); TOTP auth via /api/login + /2fa; pre-condition skip if dashboard is not in paper mode or if open paper position exists (protects operator data); for each of 4 paper commands (SET_STOP_LOSS, SET_TAKE_PROFIT, CLOSE_POSITION, SELL_ALL) POST /api/trade and assert the no-open-position error envelope (status 400 + body {ok:false, error: /^No open paper position to /}); defense-in-depth `page.context().route` interception on `api.kraken.com` capturing any browser-context request and asserting the captured list is empty.

Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (test-only addition; `tests/` not in `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md` HARD BLOCK or RESTRICTED list; `/api/data` is mode-agnostic; read-only spec; no real-money behavior change; no `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` action). Per runbook §3 (N-2t) and §4(x)(a) GAP-D Case 2 (tightened in N-2t), `railway up` deploys without a verifiable full 40-character commit SHA are REJECTED as a valid §4(x)(a) source-identity surface for N-3. Image digest, deployment ID, timestamp, and operator-attested mapping are NOT commit SHAs. **The next N-3 attempt requires a GitHub-push-tracked deploy** (or any equivalent that produces a Railway-recorded commit SHA on a non-secret surface).
- [ ] **Path Z block at HEAD `6c3a1e5`:** Path Z (N-3 against currently-deployed HEAD via Option E) is structurally blocked by the N-2t deploy-method rejection until a GitHub-tracked deploy produces a commit-SHA-recording deployment.

Pin matches the deployed runtime as captured at fact-finding time (in-container `node --version` returned `v24.10.0` from a `railway ssh` session into the production `agent-avila-dashboard` container). `package.json` `engines.node` remains the non-exact range `">=18.0.0"`, unchanged. **Verified PASS at deployed HEAD `49650f077509d83dcbf3e9771dc9ca30f351e55b` on 2026-05-04** via Web UI deploy-identity capture (GitHub-push-tracked) plus `railway ssh` in-container `node --version` = `v24.10.0` byte-for-byte match against `.nvmrc`. Freshness invalidation rule (per N-2u) remains in force.

The runbook's §4(vii) connectivity-preflight base content, the N-2x §4(vii) /app module-resolution + host/container command-boundary rule, the N-2y §4(vii) ESM/CommonJS module-format rule, the §4(x) source-revision identity / Node-runtime identity / dependency identity rules, the §4(x)(b) `.nvmrc` source-priority list / canonical-source rule / parsing-hygiene rule / freshness-invalidation rule (codified in N-2u), and HALT-on-disagreement rules remain unchanged; §4(x) is extended (not weakened) by the N-2z Evidence-record form paragraph.

## P2 Informational (post-N-2q findings)

- **`package.json:8` declares `"start": "node bot.js"` but Railway/Nixpacks runs `node dashboard.js`.** Service-side override (Railway custom start command, or Nixpacks-detected entry) reconciles the difference. Not material to Check D or §11 N-3 gate; tracked for future audit.
- **`railway shell` is NOT the approved deployed-runtime-shell surface.** `railway shell` opens a local subshell with Railway env vars injected (in-shell `node --version` returns operator's local Node, not the deployed runtime). Same REJECTED category as `railway run`, per runbook §3 N-2n rejection of local-injection surfaces. The approved primary N-3 execution surface is `railway ssh` into the deployed `agent-avila-dashboard` container; N-2o GAP-A four conditions empirically PASS for that surface. (A separately scoped runbook-tightening phase could add an explicit "rejects `railway shell`" line to runbook §3 alongside the existing `railway run` rejection; deferred.)
- **Active builder confirmed: Nixpacks v1.41.0** (Railway build log header). Nixpacks documents Node major-version control only; the deployed exact patch is determined by Nixpacks's current Node 24 selection at deploy time and is observable from in-container `node --version`. The N-2r-preflight freshness rule applies — patch may shift on future Nixpacks updates.

## P2 Informational (N-2t findings)

- **`railway up` deploys do NOT record a commit SHA on Railway's non-secret surfaces.** The currently-running production deploy of `agent-avila-dashboard` was triggered by `railway up` (per the project's documented deploy command at `package.json:14`). The Railway dashboard exposes deployment ID, image digest, build provenance, timestamp, and start command — but no commit SHA. Both Cases of §4(x)(a) GAP-D fail for the current deploy: Case 1 (in-container `git rev-parse HEAD`) returned `fatal: not a git repository` because the deployed NIXPACKS container lacks `.git/` metadata (binary `/bin/git` exists; metadata does not); Case 2 (Railway deploy-metadata fallback) returned no commit SHA.
- **N-2t codifies the rule:** deploys lacking a verifiable full 40-character commit SHA on a non-secret Railway deploy-metadata surface are REJECTED as a valid §4(x)(a) source-identity surface for N-3 (mirrors N-2n / N-2o rejection patterns for local-injection surfaces). Approved N-3 deploy path is GitHub-push-tracked deploys (or equivalent commit-SHA-recording deploys).
- **Post-N-2z + Attempt 6 sequence (historical record):** Codex re-review PASS on the N-2z four-file docs diff → operator approved N-2z commit at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877` → pushed to GitHub `origin/main` (GitHub-push-tracked deploy method per N-2t) → Railway auto-deploy of `agent-avila-dashboard` succeeded with the post-N-2z commit SHA recorded on Railway's non-secret deploy-metadata surface → operator ran Check D re-verification at the deployed HEAD via `railway ssh` (in-container `node --version` = `v24.10.0` byte-for-byte against `.nvmrc`, per the N-2u-codified §4(x)(b) freshness rule) → operator included operator-attested Check D PASS + Railway deploy-identity evidence in the Codex N-3 preflight packet (per the N-2z-codified §4(x) Evidence-record form rule) → fresh Codex N-3 preflight at HEAD `189eb1b…` returned clean PASS → Victor granted fresh production-action approval naming HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877` → **Attempt 6 SUCCEEDED** from a fresh `railway ssh` session per the N-2m same-session rule, with corrected operator-side preflight tooling discipline per the N-2x §4(vii) rule (checker scripts under `/app`, not `/tmp`; host-side commands not pasted into the railway ssh container shell) AND the N-2y §4(vii) rule (CommonJS `require(...)` scripts using `.cjs` extension or ESM `import`); runner exit 0; Migration 008 applied; `emergency_audit_log` created. Codex closeout = SUCCESS WITH NARROW DOCS-ONLY CORRECTION REQUIRED → N-2aa applies the §6(a) wording correction. **The sequence is complete; N-3 is closed; Migration 008 is APPLIED.**

## COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-DESIGN-§15-EXTENSION-FOR-PHASE-E — Closed at `c3b3fbcc107d00022beae87ff238b43e351d282c`

COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-DESIGN-§15-EXTENSION-FOR-PHASE-E is a DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed canonical-update phase that extends the canonical RUNTIME-DESIGN §15 halt-class enumeration from 28 to 32 IDs, adding 4 new entries in a new Layer 4 to support the future Phase E SAFE IMPLEMENTATION's verification gates. Source-design HEAD `0314d2c0df50b67292a1f219a8d8fcc26f693655`. The extension was prompted by Codex DESIGN-ONLY review of `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-E-VERIFY-DESIGN`: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (7 RE applied; expanded to full canonical E-VERIFY); round-2 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (3 RE applied; gate-9 split, gate-7 §15 extension flag, DPI-E10 wording); round-3 overall PASS confirming all 3 round-2 RE applied. Codex round-2 read §15 verbatim and identified 4 unbound failure modes: per-message schema mismatch (gate 1), schema-file boot failures, gate 7 placeholder violation, gate 9 hook-missing/integrity-failure. New IDs added to §15 Layer 4: 29 (per-message schema mismatch); 30 (schema file unverifiable at boot); 31 (allow-listed-placeholder violation); 32 (network allowlist hook missing/integrity-failure). Existing IDs 1-28 NOT renumbered; existing meanings NOT changed; ID 26 remains library-only. Codex round-2 §15 numeric findings preserved: gate 2=ID 3, gate 3=ID 1, gate 4=ID 2 (+ID 10 for class-auth bounds), gate 5=ID 7, gate 6=ID 8, gate 8=ID 4 (char) / ID 5 (rate), gate 9=ID 6 (egress) / ID 23 (hook bypass), gate 10=ID 10 with caveat, gate 11=ID 12. DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed phase. Scope: 4 parent-repo files (1 canonical RUNTIME-DESIGN.md update + 3 status doc updates). Pre-authorizes nothing downstream. ## COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `02edc238790c016fb5c36bc7b0fbdd563fa030f7`

COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-DESIGN` (Mode 2) report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-DESIGN.md`. Codex DESIGN-ONLY review track returned round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (10 RE applied verbatim conversation-only: RE-1 non-env injected `boot({ phaseGStubMode })`; RE-2 `relayMode`-first stub resolution; RE-3 5-step halt-class extraction precedence with fallback to halt 25; RE-4 `moveToProcessed` post-append failure → `secondaryHaltClass: 24` + `moved: false`; RE-5 boot halts stdout-only, no sentinel BOOT records; RE-6 production-no-hook → halt class 32; RE-7 all per-message halts to PUBLISH_LOG_PATH only; RE-8 Phase F does NOT implement `discord.sendMessage`; RE-9 rate-limit keyed by `(operatorPhaseId, channelName)`; RE-10 atomic-filesystem-creation lock with halt 9); round-2 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (3 RE applied verbatim: RE-A lock acquisition reordered to AFTER `validateEnv` / `createLogger` / `safeLog` binding with lock path from `validatedEnv.MESSAGE_STORE_PATH` only; RE-B two-tier boot-halt logging with pre-logger minimal synchronous stdout JSON fallback containing only `{event, haltClass, reason}` and explicit deny-list; RE-C `typeof === 'number'` numeric guard on halt-class extraction step 1 with explicit numeric guard across all override steps); round-3 narrow re-review returned overall PASS across all 23 narrow checklist goals (A1-A6, B1-B6, C1-C5, X1-X6) with no new required edits. Codex round-3 explicit non-approval note verbatim: "This is not approval to implement, commit, push, deploy, touch Discord/Railway/DB/env/production, or perform any runtime action." DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed codification phase. Scope: 4 parent-repo files (1 new SAFE-class handoff record + 3 status doc updates). Source-design HEAD `28b16d0e7b62fcf2e58421e6b6ba1185cc1381ab`. Relay-repo Phase E anchor `relentlessvic/agent-avila-relay` @ `21896d65132a1dc9d48f2f5563113c06f62d0893`. Records Phase F module breakdown: `src/index.js` entry point; `src/runtime/boot.js` boot sequencer per §4; `src/runtime/run-pipeline.js` per-message orchestrator per §6; `src/runtime/halt.js` halt-state-machine per §5; `src/runtime/pipeline-state.js` accumulator per §7; `src/runtime/rate-limit-state.js` Phase F-owned counter; `src/runtime/class-auth-counter.js` Phase F-owned counter; `src/runtime/single-instance-lock.js` atomic-filesystem-creation lock; `src/runtime/safe-log-binding.js` pre-bound 2-arg `safeLog` wrapper; `src/runtime/boot-halt-fallback.js` pre-logger synchronous stdout JSON fallback; optional `src/runtime/index.js` pure re-export aggregator (DPI-F9 — operator decision). Future mode = SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) — confirmed by Codex round-1 DPI-F7. Phase F adds zero new halt classes (consumes canonical RUNTIME-DESIGN §15 IDs 9, 20/21, 24, 25, 32 plus all gate-default IDs from Phase E). Phase boundaries: F owns orchestration + halt routing + boot orchestration + `pipelineState` + per-message Phase F state; G owns Discord gateway + network client + egress hook + production send + publish-log success record + production-send-failure halt-class binding; H owns Docker + Railway + CI + tests. No new dependency (`ajv@8.20.0` + `pino@9.14.0` from Phase B lockfile suffice; `ajv-formats` REMAINS FORBIDDEN per Phase C RE-4). Anti-features preserved: no top-level execution outside `src/index.js`; no `dotenv`; no fs writes at module load beyond lock acquisition; no network reach; no Discord client; no raw env reads for test-only options or lock path or `operatorPhaseId`; ES modules; `safeLog` mandatory post-logger; pre-logger fallback constrained to `{event, haltClass, reason}` only; Phase C/D/E-sealed files unchanged. Two operator-accepted deviations carried forward from Phase C/D/E precedent: `HaltClass` import from Phase C dropped (Phase F modules declare private `const HALT_CLASS`); `safeLog` 2-arg binding convention (Phase F itself performs pre-binding). ## ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `71af035f9a1f7489bfd663e099a15fda7439d0a7`

ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior Codex-PASS conversation-only `ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN` (Mode 2) report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN.md`. The persisted design describes how Agent Avila's workflow can move into Google Antigravity IDE WITHOUT moving authority, secrets, commits, pushes, DB access, Railway access, Discord posting, Kraken API access, trading, Relay activation, Autopilot activation, or approvals. Architectural Option B chosen per DPI-A2: Antigravity = primary day-to-day safe-work surface; Claude Code canonical session retains EXCLUSIVE control over all approvals, all secrets, all staging/committing/pushing to both repos, all RED-tier actions, and all Codex dispatches. All 10 DPI items resolved (DPI-A1 Google Antigravity IDE; DPI-A2 Option B; DPI-A3 read access agent-avila; DPI-A4 read access agent-avila-relay; DPI-A5 no memory-file access; DPI-A6 read-only CLI only; DPI-A7 no .env access; DPI-A8 Codex review required for Antigravity-generated code; DPI-A9 no auto-memory sync; DPI-A10 no commits or pushes from Antigravity). Codex DESIGN-ONLY review track returned round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (1 RE applied verbatim conversation-only: RE-1 removed `git fetch` from §10 ALLOWED list and added it to FORBIDDEN list with conditional wording "git fetch from Antigravity unless separately approved in a future operator-manual setup phase" because `git fetch` mutates local Git metadata by updating refs/remotes/origin/*, downloading pack data, and writing to FETCH_HEAD — not strictly read-only); round-2 narrow re-review returned overall PASS across all 4 ultra-narrow goals with no new required edits. DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed codification phase. Scope: 4 parent-repo files (1 new SAFE-class handoff record + 3 status doc updates). Source-design HEAD `02edc238790c016fb5c36bc7b0fbdd563fa030f7`. Authority preservation (firm; mirrors CLAUDE.md Discord-is-not-approval rule extended to Antigravity): Victor remains sole approver; Antigravity is NEVER an approval surface; only Victor's in-session chat instruction in the canonical Claude Code session grants approval; Antigravity-hosted agents cannot self-approve (ROLE-HIERARCHY.md extended); no automation-non-promotion bypass. Secret-handling rules (firm after DPI-A7): no .env access; no Railway/Discord/Kraken/DB/GitHub tokens in Antigravity context; no manual live-armed flag literal exposure in Antigravity-drafted text; git read access via SSH key only (git log/diff/status/show/ls-files allowed; git push, git commit, git fetch forbidden); no memory-file mirroring per DPI-A5; Antigravity's own credentials separate. Trading runtime boundary (firm): bot.js/dashboard.js/db.js/migrations/scripts/position.json MUST NOT be edited from Antigravity, executed from Antigravity, have secrets accessed, or have production state read. Relay / external Hermes Agent boundary (firm): Relay stays DORMANT; Antigravity may read Relay docs but cannot activate Relay or advance Stages 1-10b; external Hermes Agent (Nous/OpenRouter) NOT integrated; no Discord bot/token/webhook touch from Antigravity. Orchestrator boundary (firm): all orchestrator docs + ARC-1-7 remain authoritatively maintained in Claude Code session; Antigravity may READ but NOT write. Antigravity-generated code review rule (firm after DPI-A8): all code drafted in Antigravity must go through operator carry-back to Claude Code session, Codex on-disk review (per-phase cadence as Phases C/D/E), optional Gemini/ChatGPT review per 3-brains rule, and only then operator-approved staging/commit/push from Claude Code session. Memory-sync rule (firm after DPI-A9): no automatic memory cross-pollination; operator-mediated transfer only. CLI scope (firm; revised per Codex RE-1): ALLOWED = git status/log/diff/ls-files/show, cat/head/tail/less, grep/rg/ag/find, node/npm --version, node -e small expressions, hermetic test-suites only if explicitly whitelisted. FORBIDDEN = git push, git commit to canonical branches, git fetch (per RE-1 unless separately approved), npm install/ci/publish, node bot.js/dashboard.js, psql/pg_dump, railway, Discord CLI/curl/webhook, Kraken API CLI, any .env read/write, any memory-file write. Workspace configuration recommendations (operator-side; conversation-only): exclude rules for .env/.env.*/node_modules/~/.claude/position.json/secrets/credentials/cert files; disable auto-side-effecting agents and MCP/extensions introducing new approval surfaces; read-only credentials only (no GitHub PAT with repo:write; no SSH key with push privileges); operator workflow uses Antigravity for design/drafting/exploration with copy-back to Claude Code for canonical placement; entire workspace section is conversation-only recommendation with no install step authorized by this DESIGN-SPEC. Pre-authorizes nothing downstream. Does NOT authorize: installing Google Antigravity IDE; configuring any Antigravity workspace; giving Antigravity any credential; any Antigravity-side commit/push/deploy/Discord/Railway/DB/Kraken/trading/Relay activation/Autopilot activation/external Hermes Agent integration; modification of ARC-1-7; advancing phase-loop counter; breaking CEILING-PAUSE; modifying trading runtime; modifying orchestrator authority; posting to Discord; reading .env or memory from Antigravity; running git push/git fetch/npm install/node bot.js/psql/railway from Antigravity; Stage 5 install resumption; Stage 7 dry-run; Stages 8/9/10a/10b; DASH-6/D-5.12f/Migration 009+; memory-file edit; test-suite edit; modification of any safety-policy doc / canonical Relay handoff record / runtime / migration / script / deploy file; Phase F SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (`COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT`) or any subsequent Relay-runtime lettered phase. Codex round-2 PASS verdict does NOT constitute operator approval. Migration 008 APPLIED preserved; N-3 CLOSED preserved; Stage 5 Gate-10 install approval at `40f3137e…` CONSUMED preserved; Phase A CLOSED at Relay-repo `fcfec4882…` preserved; Phase A closeout CLOSED at parent-repo `1b20628ed…` preserved; B-DEPS-DESIGN-SPEC CLOSED at parent-repo `2b9144fc…` preserved; Phase B CLOSED at Relay-repo `f87faef99…` preserved; Phase B closeout CLOSED at parent-repo `5f2fc810c…` preserved; C-CONFIG-DESIGN-SPEC CLOSED at parent-repo `491a24f5c…` preserved; Phase C CLOSED at Relay-repo `413a4fbe…` preserved; Phase C closeout CLOSED at parent-repo `7e0d227d…` preserved; D-STORE-DESIGN-SPEC CLOSED at parent-repo `1625f13f…` preserved; Phase D CLOSED at Relay-repo `0d0210a3…` preserved; Phase D closeout CLOSED at parent-repo `0314d2c0…` preserved; §15-EXTENSION-FOR-PHASE-E CLOSED at parent-repo `c3b3fbcc…` preserved; E-VERIFY-DESIGN-SPEC CLOSED at parent-repo `a7a1f7a…` preserved; Phase E CLOSED at Relay-repo `21896d65…` preserved; E-VERIFY-CLOSEOUT CLOSED at parent-repo `28b16d0e…` preserved; F-HALT-DESIGN-SPEC CLOSED at parent-repo `02edc23…` preserved; Relay-runtime DORMANT preserved; Autopilot DORMANT (verified at `eff4dd2…`) preserved; CEILING-PAUSE history preserved; approvers exactly `{Victor}` preserved. ## ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `9d47f74d87aeed20a2fa7483a3704b494a21eb96`

ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior Codex-PASS conversation-only `ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN` (Mode 2) report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN.md` plus the actual canonical safety-rules file at `orchestrator/ANTIGRAVITY-RULES.md`. The persisted design defines an in-repo safety-rules file intended to be read by Antigravity-hosted AI agents (and any IDE-side AI agent that loads the workspace) when they first encounter the working tree. The file serves as a self-reminding agent-facing prompt that re-states the rules from CLAUDE.md + the canonical Antigravity migration design every time an Antigravity agent reads it. The file grants no new authority and is a derived restatement of existing canonical rules. Deferred from `ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-A-WORKSPACE-CONFIG-DESIGN-SPEC` DPI-WC-9. COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-CLOSEOUT records the operator-approved completion of Phase F SAFE IMPLEMENTATION in the separate Relay repo `relentlessvic/agent-avila-relay`. Phase F `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT` (SAFE IMPLEMENTATION / Mode 4) is CLOSED at the Relay-repo commit SHA `b8ab035034668fd53ea6efe64432f0868dfd2eb9` (operator-approved Claude-run commit + push to `relentlessvic/agent-avila-relay` `origin/main`; fast-forward `21896d6..b8ab035`; three-way SHA consistency PASS). Phase F added 11 runtime modules to the Relay repo (1874 LOC total): `src/index.js` (52 LOC entry point); `src/runtime/boot.js` (668 LOC 16-stage boot sequencer per Phase F design §4 with Stage 4 lock after validateEnv/createLogger/safeLog binding per RE-A; Tier-1 pre-logger fallback + Tier-2 post-logger safeLog per RE-B; Stage 15 gate-9 ref resolution per §7 firm rule); `src/runtime/run-pipeline.js` (286 LOC 11-gate orchestrator + post-gate-11 routing per §6); `src/runtime/halt.js` (228 LOC halt-state machine per §5 with 5-step strict extraction precedence per RE-3 + RE-C and append-then-move halt sequence with RE-4 failure path); `src/runtime/pipeline-state.js` (35 LOC accumulator); `src/runtime/rate-limit-state.js` (183 LOC counter keyed by `(operatorPhaseId, channelName)` per RE-9); `src/runtime/class-auth-counter.js` (55 LOC); `src/runtime/single-instance-lock.js` (227 LOC atomic `mkdir` lock per RE-10 + RE-A with POSIX `kill(pid,0)` liveness probe); `src/runtime/safe-log-binding.js` (36 LOC); `src/runtime/boot-halt-fallback.js` (61 LOC Tier-1 pre-logger stdout JSON fallback per RE-B); `src/runtime/index.js` (43 LOC aggregator per DPI-F9). Codex on-disk source review history: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (18 of 20 goals PASS; Goal 7 FAIL on pre-Stage-1 "Stage 0" `phaseGStubMode` validation block deviation; Goal 15 FAIL on forbidden-term comment references); RE-1 applied (removed Stage 0 block + dead `ALLOWED_PHASE_G_STUB_MODES` constant; `boot.js` 683 → 668 LOC); RE-2 applied (comment-only rewordings in 4 files replacing `Discord`/`discord.sendMessage`/`DB`/`Kraken`/`Railway`/`trading` with neutral phrasings); narrow round-2 returned overall PASS with no new required edits. Phase F is wired but NOT activated: production path fails closed when `src/index.js` invokes `boot()` bare (no `operatorPhaseId` → `createRateLimitState` throws at Stage 13 → boot halts; no Phase G hook → Stage 15 halts class 32); dry-run path requires test-harness invocation. Phase C/D/E sealed-file discipline preserved (no sealed-file touch). No `package.json`/`package-lock.json` modifications. No new dependencies. No platform send-message API client; no production publish-log success record write; no production-send-failure halt-class binding; no network reach; no database client; no exchange API; no deploy command; no env mutation; no manual live-armed flag touch; no `bot.js`/`dashboard.js`/`position.json` references; no literal manual live-armed flag occurrences. Operator-manual execution preserved per canonical operator-only pattern (Claude-direct Write workflow per DPI-F-IMPL-2; matches Phase D + Phase E operator-delegation precedent). ## COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `f7d511c31f36b6d39b2b7cfe79cba9c8e31d10ee`

COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v5 report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN.md`. The persisted design covers a future SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT` whose scope is exactly 1 Relay-repo file (`src/runtime/boot.js`), 5 hunks, ~30 lines, fixing four confirmed Phase F interface-contract drifts between `boot.js` and the sealed Phase C/D/E factories it consumes: (Bug 1) `validateEnv` returns `{ env }` but `boot.js:139-142` destructures `{ parsed }` causing fail-loud TypeError pre-Stage 2; (Bug 2) `createOperatorAuthorizationGate` (sealed Phase E factory at `operator-authorization.js:72-76`) requires `stalenessThresholdMs` but `boot.js:374-381` (Stage 12) + `boot.js:481-488` (Stage 14) pass ignored `classAuthorizations` causing fail-silent per-message staleness bypass with class-auth path unaffected; (Bug 3) `createLimitsGate` (sealed Phase E factory at `limits.js:56-61`) requires `channelRateLimits` dereferenced at `limits.js:79` but `boot.js:401-408` (Stage 12) + `boot.js:489-496` (Stage 14) omit it causing fail-loud TypeError on per-message `verify()`; (Bug 4) `limits.js:47-50,81-82` consumer documents/consumes `getRateLimitState()` → channel→count map but sealed Phase D producer `rate-limit-state.js:5,116-157` implements `({ channelName }) → status object`, and `boot.js:494` directly pass-throughs the producer without an adapter causing fail-silent rate-limit bypass (halt class 5 `RATE_LIMIT_HIT` never fires regardless of message volume). All four bugs were independently confirmed via READ-ONLY AUDITs against sealed source. Codex DESIGN-ONLY rounds 3 + 4 + 5 history: round-3 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS surfacing Bug 4 callable-shape drift at Goal 17 + parent-HEAD anchor mismatch at Goal 19 (expanded v2 → v3 → v4); round-4 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS at Goal 11 requiring two documentation halt-class corrections to §12 + §14 (applied to v5); round-5 first dispatch mixed (substantive PASS on halt-class numbers verified against sealed `HALT_CLASS = Object.freeze({...})` constants in `limits.js:25-28`, `operator-authorization.js:38-41`, `boot.js:105-116`, `config.js:28-31`, `rate-limit-state.js:38-40`; procedural FAIL because v5 was intentionally conversation-only with no persisted file); round-5 clarification resume overall PASS (procedural FAIL withdrawn; Changes A–E confirmed structurally sound and unchanged from v4; parent HEAD discrepancy confirmed reconciled). Parent HEAD reconciliation completed via READ-ONLY AUDIT: session-start gitStatus `51662f3…` was an ancestor 18 commits behind current `4a0e5518…`; no commits lost; stale snapshot. Five changes covered verbatim: Change A Stage 1 validateEnv destructure (2 lines); Change B Stage 12 createOperatorAuthorizationGate with `stalenessThresholdMs` from `runtimeConfig.operatorAuthorizationStalenessThresholdMs` + 24h fallback (~5 lines); Change C Stage 14 createOperatorAuthorizationGate re-bind mirror with `classAuthCounter.getClassAuthorizationUseCount` wired (~5 lines); Change D Stage 12 createLimitsGate adds `channelRateLimits` from `runtimeConfig.channelRateLimits || {}` (~4 lines); Change E Stage 14 createLimitsGate re-bind adds `channelRateLimits` + `getRateLimitState` adapter closure that iterates `Object.keys(runtimeConfig.channelRateLimits || {})` and calls producer's `rateLimitState.getRateLimitState({ channelName })` per channel extracting `.count` to build the consumer's channel→count map (~14 lines). Halt-class wiring unchanged; restores correct behavior for already-defined canonical §15 IDs 2, 4, 5, 10, 20, 21. DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) codification phase. Scope: 4 parent-repo files (1 new SAFE-class handoff record + 3 status doc updates `orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`). ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `f6aaa409889ec76632f8b80e9954d1cb38b178a9`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v2 report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-DESIGN.md`. The persisted design covers a future SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT` to author a read-only generator script plus an initial `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` consolidating active phase, completed phases, paused phases, blocked phases, backlog, safety gates, dormant-vs-active systems, repo anchors, and next safe action from canonical orchestrator markdown docs and local read-only git ops only. Zero-network policy enforced (no `git ls-remote`; no remote lookups; no platform API; no Railway / DB / Discord / scheduler / cron / webhook / MCP / network call of any kind). Never reads `bot.js`, `dashboard.js`, `db.js`, `position.json*`, `.env*`, secrets, `.nvmrc`, `railway.json`, deploy scripts, CI/CD config, Claude settings / hooks / MCP / slash-command permission files, live Kraken / exchange paths, production DB clients / queries, Discord bot / token / webhook / scheduler surfaces, or Relay-repo state. Never writes any file at runtime; refresh is a separately-gated DOCS-ONLY phase. Two-dimensional state model: phase status (CLOSED / ACTIVE / PAUSED / DESIGNED-NOT-OPENED / BACKLOG-DESIGNED / BACKLOG-IDEA / BLOCKED-DEPENDENCY / BLOCKED-DECISION / DORMANT) separated from protection class (SAFE / RESTRICTED / HARD BLOCK per PROTECTED-FILES.md). Recommended implementation Option A: static markdown regeneration (read-only Node script emits to stdout; operator pipes to file via `>` and explicitly stages; never writes any file itself). Codex DESIGN-ONLY review history: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS — 12 required edits applied verbatim; round-2 narrow re-review overall PASS across all 18 narrow goals with no further required edits. ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `c8798ea94b1b7cf55a589a253e99e5d5476178de`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v2 report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN.md`. The persisted implementation design covers a future SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT` to author a read-only Node generator script (recommended path `tools/dashboard-generate.js`, subject to Codex implementation review at Mode 4 implementation time) plus an initial `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` consistent with the canonical dashboard DESIGN handoff at `f6aaa40…` (sealed; not modified by this codification). Stdout-only generator; never writes files at runtime; operator pipes via `>` and stages explicitly. Zero-network policy: no remote lookups; no `git ls-remote`; no platform API; no Railway/DB/Discord/scheduler/cron/webhook/MCP/network call. No `process.env` reads at all; the future generator must not read environment variables. Three internal layers (data/parse/render). Allowed local read paths (12 items: STATUS.md, CHECKLIST.md, NEXT-ACTION.md, handoffs/*.md, AUTOPILOT-RULES.md, APPROVAL-GATES.md, PHASE-MODES.md, PROTECTED-FILES.md, COMM-HUB-RULES.md, COMM-HUB-RELAY-RULES.md, COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-DESIGN.md, CLAUDE.md). Refused/forbidden paths (deny-by-default allowlist; covers bot.js, dashboard.js, db.js, migrations/*, scripts/*, package*.json, position.json*, .env*, .nvmrc, railway.json, secrets, Relay repo paths, memory files, test files, exchange paths, production DB clients/queries, Discord bot/token/webhook/scheduler surfaces, Claude settings/hooks/MCP/slash-command permission files, Railway/DB/external API endpoints). Allowed git commands (4 only): `git log --oneline -N`, `git rev-parse HEAD`, `git rev-parse origin/main`, `git status --short`. Forbidden git commands: `git ls-remote` (network), `git fetch`/`pull`/`push` (network/write), `git checkout`/`reset`/`merge`/`rebase` (write), `git commit`/`add`/`tag`/`stash` (write). Parse-failure: fail loud (exit non-zero with stderr structured error); no partial dashboard. Initial `DASHBOARD.md` output: header metadata + 11 content sections per canonical DESIGN §9 (canonical DESIGN §4 is a 10-row summary, not the authoritative section count); approximate length 80-120 lines; `bot.js`/`dashboard.js` labeled OFF-SCOPE/NOT ASSESSED BY THIS DASHBOARD. 5-file future implementation scope: NEW `tools/dashboard-generate.js` + NEW `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` + MODIFIED STATUS.md / CHECKLIST.md / NEXT-ACTION.md. Test scope: no new test files in v1 implementation scope; existing validators may be run per CLAUDE.md §Change discipline and PHASE-MODES.md Mode 4; `npm install`/`npm ci` remains out of scope unless separately gated; future `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-TESTS-DESIGN` phase can design new unit tests later if desired. Codex DESIGN-ONLY review history: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS — 4 required edits applied verbatim; round-2 narrow re-review overall PASS across all 13 narrow goals with no further required edits. PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT is the SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase authoring the read-only Project Progress Dashboard generator per the sealed canonical handoffs at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-DESIGN.md` (codified at `f6aaa40…`) and `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN.md` (codified at `c8798ea…`). 5-file scope: NEW `tools/dashboard-generate.js` (~400 LOC Node ES modules, Node built-ins only — `fs.readFileSync`, `path`, `child_process.execFileSync`, `url`); NEW `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` (~126 lines initial output captured via operator-approved `node tools/dashboard-generate.js > orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` redirection; script never writes files itself); MODIFIED `orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`. Generator hard guarantees verified by code: no write-side fs API imported; no network module imports; no `child_process` invocation outside the 4 read-only git commands (`git log --oneline -N`, `git rev-parse HEAD`, `git rev-parse origin/main`, `git status --short`); no `process.env` reads anywhere. Explicit path allowlist (12 canonical orchestrator markdown paths + `orchestrator/handoffs/*.md` glob); explicit git command allowlist; fail-loud on parse/allowlist/command failure (exit non-zero with stderr `DashboardError: <reason> at <context>`); no partial dashboard emission. Initial `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` contains 11 content sections + header metadata per canonical DESIGN §9: header (Generated timestamp; Parent HEAD `7b7e1cc` in sync with origin/main; Relay HEAD `b8ab035` Phase F sealed; working-tree note); Where Are We Now; Active Phase; Safety Gates table (10 gates); Completed Phases (26 entries); Paused Phases; Designed/Not-Opened; Backlog/Future Ideas (14 items); Phase Timeline/Roadmap (committed-anchored only); Repo Anchors; Dormant vs Active Systems (`bot.js`/`dashboard.js` OFF-SCOPE per RE-1/RE-2); Next Safe Action. ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-SCAFFOLD — Closed at `cc6819d46689e448911cac2c200113cd76670c74`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-SCAFFOLD is the first Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION phase in the WEB cascade, recorded retrospectively. ## COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN-DESIGN-SPEC — CLOSED at `5acac86b521b8e3783b43018d4091194316e7a61`

COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN-DESIGN-SPEC is a DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed codification phase persisting the accepted conversation-only `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) as a permanent SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN-DESIGN.md`. The codified handoff records the byte-identity smoke-run plan that, when later executed under a separately-gated SAFE EXECUTION (Mode 4) phase with explicit Victor approval, would run the 11 contract-aligned smoke tests against sealed Relay source at `590c1c9b42d96298c625df17ad892e7bf318c8ab` and produce three artifacts: (a) captured TAP stream at `orchestrator/handoffs/evidence/F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN/tap-run-1.txt`; (b) pre/post SHA-256 byte-identity captures of the `tests/smoke/` tree at `tests-smoke-before.sha256` / `tests-smoke-after.sha256`; (c) sealed-state verification before and after via `git rev-parse HEAD && git status --porcelain` on both repos. The codified §3 byte-identity commands enforce: sequential test execution via `--test-concurrency=1` on every `node --test` invocation; explicit working directory `/Users/victormercado/code/agent-avila-relay`; explicit redirection target paths; explicit `find tests/smoke -type f -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 shasum -a 256` pipeline for deterministic byte-identity capture; explicit `diff -u` compare with zero-byte expected output. The codified §5 post-execution gates enforce PASS criteria (all four required): §5.1 sealed-anchor unchanged AND §5.1a byte-identity diff empty AND §5.2 TAP tally `12 / 11 pass / 1 skip / 0 fail` AND §5.3 working tree clean. §5.1a is a hard-FAIL gate: any non-empty diff is automatic FAIL even if TAP reports `# fail 0`. Case 12 remains `test.skip()` + in-file TODO citing `src/verify/limits.js:83` channelCap object-vs-`maxPerWindow` drift; remediation Option B (component-level test rewrite) and Option C (Phase E `limits.js:83` source amendment) are separately gated. Codex review history: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS on (a) `--test-concurrency=1` mandatory on every `node --test` invocation, (b) byte-identity commands quoted with explicit pwd / flags / redirection / glob form — operator applied verbatim. SCAFFOLD-REPAIR scope (Relay-repo): exactly 6 files modified — 3 helpers (`tests/smoke/helpers/temp-tree.js`, `tests/smoke/helpers/synthetic-runtime-config.js`, `tests/smoke/helpers/synthetic-message.js`) + 3 smoke tests (`tests/smoke/06-gate-5-idempotency-collision-halt.test.js` as 1-line literal replacement, `tests/smoke/07-halt-publish-log-append.test.js` as whole-file non-boot rewrite, `tests/smoke/08-pending-move-after-halt.test.js` as whole-file non-boot rewrite); 98 insertions(+), 161 deletions(-); zero `src/` touch, zero `schemas/` touch, zero `package*.json` byte change, zero parent-repo touch during implementation, zero env / secrets touch, zero test execution this phase, zero deploy. Implementation details: temp-tree.js adds `await fs.writeFile(ceilingPauseSignal, 'ACTIVE');` after AMENDMENT-4 writeFile calls (ceiling-pause-signal fixture, +1 line, AMENDMENT-3 sealPending helper preserved verbatim); synthetic-runtime-config.js adds `import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';` + `schemaPath: fileURLToPath(new URL('../../../schemas/hermes-message.schema.json', import.meta.url))` and flips `allowedChannels` to object map `{ '#status': '123456789012345678' }` (matching `src/verify/channel-allowlist.js:54`/`:62` contract); synthetic-message.js rewrite makes `shortMessageId()` emit uppercase IDs `SMOKE-<8-hex-upper>` matching schema pattern `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9-]{4,63}$`, rewrites `buildSyntheticMessage` default to satisfy 9 required schema fields with `additionalProperties: false` honored, and updates 3 variants to each apply exactly ONE targeted invalidity (delete `body` → gate-1 halt 29; `channel_name: '#summaries'` → gate-2 halt 3; 25h-old `approved_at` → gate-4 halt 2); Case 06 changes ONLY the message_id override from `'msg-collision-x'` to `'MSG-COLLISION-X'` (uppercase schema-valid) — preserves AMENDMENT-3 `await tempTree.sealPending();` + boot import + env mutation + pre-populated publish-log success record + `caught.code === 7` halt-class-7 assertion verbatim; Case 07 + Case 08 whole-file rewrites bypass `boot()` and `sealPending()` per Path D (Case 07 directly invokes `createPublishLogWriter().ensureLogIntegrity()` + `.append(haltRecord)` and asserts canonical §14 6-field shape per `src/store/publish-log.js:194-197`; Case 08 directly invokes `createMessageStore().moveToProcessed()` happy-path + sub-case forces rename failure via `fs.chmod(processed, 0o500)` and asserts `StoreError(haltClass:24, reason:/^rename-failed/)` per `src/store/source-of-truth.js:186-191`). ## COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-AMENDMENT-7-DESIGN-SPEC — CLOSED at `90d97114615eecb9e5304bd76450a3019f6caff7`

COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-AMENDMENT-7-DESIGN-SPEC is a DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed codification phase persisting the Codex-approved conversation-only `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-AMENDMENT-7-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) as a permanent SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-SMOKE-AMENDMENT-7-DESIGN.md`. The Layer 3 fix addresses the structural conflict between AMENDMENT-3 sealed-pending test fixture (`tests/smoke/helpers/temp-tree.js:30-31` chmods `pending/` to `0o555`) and the halt.js RE-4 post-halt cleanup contract (`src/runtime/halt.js:182-190` calls `store.moveToProcessed(filename)` after gate-pipeline halts; failure emits `moved:false, secondaryHaltClass:24`) that caused RUN-6 Cases 4, 5, 6, 11 to fail despite firing their correct halt classes (29, 3, 7, 2).
